products.menuLine: El Dorado 12 Year · Guyana · $38 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: molasses, toffee

guidance.bestFor: Rum explorer, toffee-molasses profile, maduro pairing.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants light white rum only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when demerara depth should meet a maduro — molasses, toffee, and orange peel.
guidance.memberBlurb: El Dorado 12 — demerara molasses and toffee from Guyanese stills. Rum with weight for members who found Zacapa too pretty.

products.menuLine: Espolòn Reposado · Jalisco, Mexico · $28 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: pepper, vanilla

guidance.bestFor: Tequila beginner, value pour, medium cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants quality reposado without Clase Azul pricing.
guidance.memberBlurb: Espolòn Reposado — pepper and vanilla agave without the luxury markup. Honest tequila that still respects a cigar.

products.menuLine: Fortaleza Reposado · Jalisco, Mexico · $55 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: cooked agave, butter

guidance.bestFor: Tequila enthusiast, still-strength agave, medium-full cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants real agave character — earth, pepper, and cooked agave.
guidance.memberBlurb: Fortaleza Reposado — cooked agave and pepper from stone ovens. Tequila for members past ceramic bottles and into flavor.

products.menuLine: Glenfiddich 12 Year · Speyside, Scotland · $48 · 80 proof (40% ABV) · Notes: pear, green apple

guidance.bestFor: First Scotch, mild cigar, user intimidated by peat or smoke.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants heavy peat or cask-strength intensity.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend as beginner Scotch — pear, honey, and gentle oak without smoke.
guidance.memberBlurb: Glenfiddich 12 — pear and butterscotch over cream, malt, and subtle oak. Official Speyside first Scotch without peat.

products.menuLine: Glenlivet 12 Year · Speyside, Scotland · $48 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: pear, apple

guidance.bestFor: First Scotch, mild cigar, Speyside flight.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants peat or bold flavor.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend as friendly Speyside beside a mild cigar — pear, apple, and honey.
guidance.memberBlurb: Glenlivet 12 — pear and floral honey in an easy Speyside. The other beginner Scotch every lounge should have next to Glenfiddich.

products.menuLine: Glenmorangie Original 10 Year · Highland, Scotland · $42 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: citrus, peach

guidance.bestFor: Delicate pairing, citrus-forward Scotch, Connecticut cigar.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants smoke or sherry bomb.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when a delicate cigar needs a delicate Scotch — citrus, cream, and vanilla.
guidance.memberBlurb: Glenmorangie Original — citrus and cream from tall stills. Delicate Highland for a Connecticut wrapper when you want finesse.

products.menuLine: Grey Goose Vodka · France · $35 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: smooth, sweet grain

guidance.avoidIf: User wants craft funky spirits only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when luxury vodka is the ask — smooth, almond, and citrus.
guidance.memberBlurb: Grey Goose — smooth winter wheat with almond and citrus. The vodka name guests request when they do not know what else to order.

products.menuLine: Hakushu 12 Year · Japan · $150 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: green apple, pear

guidance.bestFor: Japanese malt fan, smoky but elegant, medium-full cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants Japanese smoke — mint, green fruit, and forest peat.
guidance.memberBlurb: Hakushu 12 — forest smoke and mint over green apple. Japanese peat for members who found Islay too rough.
